trigger_name: Respiratory symptoms at arrival or registration
logic_definition: New arrival reports cough, fever, runny nose, sore throat, myalgia, or other compatible respiratory symptoms
rationale: This is the first operational source-control trigger and should act before admission or waiting-area exposure expands
recommended_source_basis: CDC Flu Infection Control 2025

trigger_name: Suspected or confirmed inpatient influenza
logic_definition: New inpatient suspicion or confirmation of influenza based on symptoms, clinician judgment, or testing
rationale: This is the main inpatient influenza trigger and should immediately route isolation, rooming, and treatment review
recommended_source_basis: CDC Flu Infection Control 2025

trigger_name: Same-room exposure to confirmed case
logic_definition: Patient shared the same room with a confirmed influenza case during the configured exposure window
rationale: This is one of the strongest operationally actionable exposure signals and is central to CodeBlue’s protect-the-clean-vulnerable-zone logic
recommended_source_basis: Cho 2024 plus internal CodeBlue adaptive policy bridge

trigger_name: Same-unit exposure to confirmed case
logic_definition: Patient co-located on the same ward or unit with a confirmed influenza case during the configured exposure window
rationale: This is the ward-level exposure trigger for broader surveillance and early testing when same-room data are absent or spread may already be broader
recommended_source_basis: Cho 2024 plus internal CodeBlue adaptive policy bridge

trigger_name: Single room unavailable for influenza case
logic_definition: Suspected or confirmed influenza patient cannot be placed immediately in a single room or fully preferred placement
rationale: This exists so the system routes into an approved fallback bundle rather than doing nothing when ideal IPC placement is impossible
recommended_source_basis: CDC Flu Infection Control 2025 plus contingency-rooming logic from Cazzorla 2022

trigger_name: Ill healthcare worker with fever and respiratory symptoms
logic_definition: HCP develops febrile respiratory illness before or during work and may expose patients or coworkers
rationale: This is the main staff-vector trigger and should route immediate human review rather than passive monitoring
recommended_source_basis: CDC Flu Infection Control 2025 plus CDC Core Practices 2024

trigger_name: HCP returns with residual respiratory symptoms
logic_definition: HCP has returned to work after fever resolution but still has cough or sneezing
rationale: This is a narrower follow-on trigger for ongoing source-control after return, especially in patient-facing settings
recommended_source_basis: CDC Flu Infection Control 2025

trigger_name: Exposed HCP assigned to high-vulnerability ward
logic_definition: Exposed or recently ill HCP is scheduled to work in oncology, transplant, hematology, ICU, neonatal, or other high-vulnerability service
rationale: This captures one of CodeBlue’s core adaptive criteria: preventing staff-mediated movement of pathogen into still-clean vulnerable zones
recommended_source_basis: Internal CodeBlue policy logic supported by CDC HCP management principles

trigger_name: Ward cluster signal
logic_definition: Two or more likely linked healthcare-associated influenza cases are detected on the same ward or unit within a short rolling window
rationale: This is the main ward-level outbreak trigger and should shift the system from individual review to coordinated service-level response
recommended_source_basis: CDC Flu Infection Control 2025 plus internal outbreak-governance logic

trigger_name: Transport or movement of influenza patient
logic_definition: Patient under influenza precautions must leave room for transfer, imaging, procedure, or inter-facility movement
rationale: This trigger captures a common leakage point where spread can occur unless precautions and handoff are maintained
recommended_source_basis: CDC Flu Infection Control 2025

trigger_name: Visitor request while patient remains infectious
logic_definition: In-person visit is requested while the patient remains in the infectious period for influenza
rationale: Visitor flow needs explicit review because visitation can amplify exposure risk in vulnerable wards and during clusters
recommended_source_basis: CDC Flu Infection Control 2025 plus CDC Core Practices 2024

trigger_name: Planned or emergent aerosol-generating procedure on influenza patient
logic_definition: Bronchoscopy, intubation, CPR, suctioning, or similar AGP is planned or occurring on suspected or confirmed influenza patient
rationale: AGPs create a different exposure class and require enhanced procedural review rather than ordinary bedside precautions
recommended_source_basis: CDC Flu Infection Control 2025

trigger_name: Treatment or chemoprophylaxis consideration
logic_definition: Influenza treatment or post-exposure prophylaxis review is clinically indicated for a patient or exposed person
rationale: This is the antiviral decision bridge, but it remains review-only and never auto-prescribes medication
recommended_source_basis: CDC Antivirals 2026

trigger_name: Persistent symptoms or shedding beyond default window
logic_definition: Persistent symptoms, repeated positive testing, or strong clinical concern suggests prolonged infectiousness beyond the default influenza window
rationale: This is the time-aware trigger that prevents default timing rules from prematurely downgrading complex cases
recommended_source_basis: CDC Flu Infection Control 2025 plus influenza prolonged-shedding evidence already routed into the pack

trigger_name: HCP assignment to protective-environment patients after recent influenza illness
logic_definition: HCP with recent influenza symptoms, recovery, or recent exposure may return to care for HSCT or other protective-environment patients
rationale: This is a special-population exception where stricter review is justified because downstream consequences are disproportionate
recommended_source_basis: CDC Flu Infection Control 2025 plus CDC protective-environment exception logic

trigger_name: Manual clinician or IPC override
logic_definition: Clinician, unit lead, or IPC specialist manually flags a patient, room, staff member, or ward for review
rationale: Preserves human-in-the-loop control, auditability, and the ability to act on context the automated layer has not captured
recommended_source_basis: Internal CodeBlue governance rule

trigger_name: Deployment pre-alert window active
logic_definition: Current date is on or after the local pre-alert start month and before the high-alert start month
rationale: This gives the hospital a configurable early-vigilance phase before the local peak begins
recommended_source_basis: Local deployment profile plus hospital governance

trigger_name: Deployment high-alert window active
logic_definition: Current date falls inside the locally defined high-alert influenza window
rationale: This is the configurable local seasonal activation trigger and replaces hardcoded state logic
recommended_source_basis: Local deployment profile plus official regional seasonality source

trigger_name: Elevated current community activity
logic_definition: Current community influenza activity has been set to moderate or high
rationale: This lets the hospital intensify action based on current local epidemiology rather than calendar alone
recommended_source_basis: Current epidemiology status feed

trigger_name: Elevated current hospital respiratory or influenza burden
logic_definition: Hospital respiratory burden or influenza-specific burden has been set to moderate or high
rationale: This captures live hospital pressure and prevents overreliance on seasonality calendars
recommended_source_basis: Current hospital epidemiology input

trigger_name: Manual epidemiology override
logic_definition: Active override flag is true because local epidemiology is atypical or off-season activity is under review
rationale: This preserves human control when local patterns diverge from the normal seasonal profile
recommended_source_basis: Current epidemiology status with human review
